hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity with ECL2/TM5 while avoiding off-target interactions, thereby increasing the selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving reasonable selectivity (Δmargin > 0), but structural issues are preventing them from passing QC. The focus should remain on maintaining selectivity while improving structural quality through tighter clash constraints and increased sampling.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reducing the clash tolerance will enforce better geometry and reduce structural artifacts, which are likely the main reason for the low pLDDT and docking scores.
Increasing the number of candidates will improve the chances of generating at least one high-quality, structurally valid candidate that also maintains selectivity.
